summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing for Covered IT! v2. 41/41 backend pytest tests PASSED (auth, products, phone-models, coupons, orders, analytics, webhooks, settings, reels). Full frontend e2e flow verified end-to-end: landing → her/him/collab/charms grids → product page (model picker required, add-to-cart disabled until model selected) → buy now → checkout → MAINCHAR10 coupon (₹649 → ₹584.1) → place order → success screen with AWB 'SRDE56E490T' → track page with 4-step progress. Admin login (admin@coveredit.in / admin123) lands on /admin dashboard. All admin tabs (dashboard, products, orders, customers, coupons, reels, models, settings) render without crashes. Phone models admin shows all 8 brands grouped (91 model toggles in new-product modal — close to seeded 80, confirming compatibility). Two minor UI cosmetic issues found.

ui_bugs.issue: 'CONFIRMED' renders as 'CONRRMED' — the 'FI' substring is being replaced by an OpenType ligature glyph that doesn't render in the display font when combined with uppercase + tracking. Visible on /track/{id} (step label under Lock icon) and on /admin dashboard 'CONRRMED' stat card. Fix: add `font-variant-ligatures: none;` (or `font-feature-settings: 'liga' 0, 'dlig' 0`) on display/uppercase labels.

Empty string passed to img `src` attribute (React warning x2 in console): 'An empty string ("") was passed to the %s attribute. This may cause the browser to download the whole page again'. Guard with `src={thumbnail || undefined}` or skip rendering when empty.

issues: Confirmed step label clipped by Lock icon overlap (ligature artifact, same root cause as above)

action_items: Cosmetic: disable OpenType ligatures on uppercase display labels (`font-variant-ligatures: none`) — fixes 'CONFIRMED'→'CONRRMED' on /track and /admin dashboard.
Guard against empty img src in reel/product thumbnails to silence the 2 React warnings.

NOTE for next testing agent: review request stated checkout form testids as `form-name/form-phone/form-email/...` but the actual testids are `ship-name/ship-phone/ship-email/ship-pincode/ship-addr1/ship-addr2/ship-city/ship-state`. Backend tests + my playwright scripts use the correct ones.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 905 lines — borderline acceptable; consider splitting routers (auth, products, orders, admin) into modules if it grows further.
_shiprocket_create_shipment is intentionally mocked (returns awb starting with 'SR') — MOCKED as expected by problem statement; preserved.
POST /api/orders is public (no auth) — correct for guest checkout, but consider rate-limiting in production.
GET /api/orders/{oid} is also public — anyone with an order id can read full address + items. For production, gate by email-on-order or short-lived token. Acceptable for MVP.

Coupon apply inside create_order silently swallows 4xx (try/except HTTPException: pass) — invalid coupon at checkout just becomes discount=0 with no signal to client. Consider returning a warning field. Minor.
All endpoints exclude _id correctly. Bcrypt hashes used. JWT secret from env. seed_admin idempotently resets admin password if changed. Good practices.

success_rate.frontend: 98% (all critical flows pass; 2 minor cosmetic issues)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@coveredit.in / admin123 — works. JWT + httpOnly cookie both verified.
seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_* orders via e2e + pytest (TEST_E2E, TEST_User, TEST_Low). Created and deleted a TEST_Pytest Case product. Created and deleted a TEST_Brand phone model. All test data prefixed with TEST_ as per guidelines.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_coveredit_backend.py — pytest.ini at /app/backend/pytest.ini runs them in 2-worker xdist loadscope mode. Run with: `cd /app/backend && pytest tests/test_coveredit_backend.py -v`. Frontend checkout form testids are `ship-name/ship-phone/ship-email/ship-pincode/ship-addr1/ship-addr2/ship-city/ship-state` (NOT `form-*` as in original brief). Cart context key is localStorage 'ci_cart_v1'; auth token key is 'ci_token'. Shiprocket stub returns awb 'SR' + first 8 of order id uppercased.
rca of the issue: Frontend 'CONRRMED' rendering: when the display font is rendered uppercase with letter-spacing/tracking, the 'FI' digraph hits an OpenType discretionary ligature that has no glyph in the loaded font subset, so the 2 chars vanish leaving 'CON_RMED'. Mitigation: disable ligatures on .text-uppercase / display headings with `font-variant-ligatures: none` or set `font-feature-settings: 'liga' 0`.
